THE FACTORIES ACT, 1948
ACT NO. 63 OF 1948<sup>1</sup>
\[23rd September, 1948.]
items.value:
THE FACTORIES ACT, 1948
ACT NO. 63 OF 1948<sup>1</sup>
[23rd September, 1948.]

items.md: An Act to consolidate and amend the law regulating labour in factories.
items.value: An Act to consolidate and amend the law regulating labour in factories.

items.md: WHEREAS it is expedient to consolidate and amend the law regulating labour in factories;
items.value: WHEREAS it is expedient to consolidate and amend the law regulating labour in factories;

items.md: It is hereby enacted as follows:—
items.value: It is hereby enacted as follows:—

items.md: # CHAPTER I
items.value: CHAPTER I

items.md: # PRELIMINARY
items.value: PRELIMINARY

items.md: **1. Short title, extent and commencement.**—(1) This Act may be called the Factories Act, 1948.
items.value: 1. Short title, extent and commencement.—(1) This Act may be called the Factories Act, 1948.

items.md: <sup>2</sup>\[(2) It extends to the whole of India <sup>3</sup>\*\*\*.
items.value: <sup>2</sup>[(2) It extends to the whole of India <sup>3</sup>***.

items.md: (3) It shall come into force on the 1st day of April 1949.
items.value: (3) It shall come into force on the 1st day of April 1949.

items.md: **2. Interpretation.**—In this Act, unless there is anything repugnant in the subject or context,—
items.value: 2. Interpretation.—In this Act, unless there is anything repugnant in the subject or context,—

items.md: (a) “adult” means a person who has completed his eighteenth year of age;
items.value: (a) “adult” means a person who has completed his eighteenth year of age;

items.md: (b) “adolescent” means a person who has completed his fifteenth year of age but has not completed his eighteenth year;
items.value: (b) “adolescent” means a person who has completed his fifteenth year of age but has not completed his eighteenth year;

items.md: <sup>4</sup>\[(bb) “calendar year” means the period of twelve months beginning with the first day of January in any year;]
items.value: <sup>4</sup>[(bb) “calendar year” means the period of twelve months beginning with the first day of January in any year;]

items.md: (c) “child” means a person who has not completed his fifteenth year of age;
items.value: (c) “child” means a person who has not completed his fifteenth year of age;

items.md: <sup>5</sup>\[(ca) “competent person”, in relation to any provision of this Act, means a person or an institution recognised as such by the Chief Inspector for the purposes of carrying out tests, examinations and inspection required to be done in a factory under the provisions of this Act having regard to—
items.value: <sup>5</sup>[(ca) “competent person”, in relation to any provision of this Act, means a person or an institution recognised as such by the Chief Inspector for the purposes of carrying out tests, examinations and inspection required to be done in a factory under the provisions of this Act having regard to—

items.md: (i) the qualifications and experience of the person and facilities available at his disposal; or
items.value: (i) the qualifications and experience of the person and facilities available at his disposal; or

items.md: (ii) the qualifications and experience of the persons employed in such institution and facilities available therein,
items.value: (ii) the qualifications and experience of the persons employed in such institution and facilities available therein,

items.md: with regard to the conduct of such tests, examinations and inspections, and more than one person or institution can be recognised as a competent person in relation to a factory;
items.value: with regard to the conduct of such tests, examinations and inspections, and more than one person or institution can be recognised as a competent person in relation to a factory;

items.md: (cb) “hazardous process” means any process or activity in relation to an industry specified in the First Schedule where, unless special care is taken, raw materials used therein or the intermediate or finished products, bye-products, wastes or effluents thereof would—
items.value: (cb) “hazardous process” means any process or activity in relation to an industry specified in the First Schedule where, unless special care is taken, raw materials used therein or the intermediate or finished products, bye-products, wastes or effluents thereof would—

items.md: (i) cause material impairment to the health of the persons engaged in or connected therewith, or
items.value: (i) cause material impairment to the health of the persons engaged in or connected therewith, or

items.md: (ii) result in the pollution or the general environment:
items.value: (ii) result in the pollution or the general environment:

items.md: Provided that the State Government may, by notification in the Official Gazette, amend the First Schedule by way of addition, omission or variation of any industry specified in the said Schedule;
items.value: Provided that the State Government may, by notification in the Official Gazette, amend the First Schedule by way of addition, omission or variation of any industry specified in the said Schedule;

items.md: (d) “young person” means a person who is either a child or an adolescent;
items.value: (d) “young person” means a person who is either a child or an adolescent;

items.md: (e) “day” means a period of twenty-four hours beginning at midnight:
items.value: (e) “day” means a period of twenty-four hours beginning at midnight:

items.md: (f) “week” means a period of seven days beginning at midnight on Saturday night or such other night as may be approved in writing for a particular area by the Chief Inspector of Factories;
items.value: (f) “week” means a period of seven days beginning at midnight on Saturday night or such other night as may be approved in writing for a particular area by the Chief Inspector of Factories;

items.md: (g) “power” means electrical energy or any other form of energy which is mechanically transmitted and is not generated by human or animal agency;
items.value: (g) “power” means electrical energy or any other form of energy which is mechanically transmitted and is not generated by human or animal agency;

items.md: **<sup>1</sup>\[4. Power to declare different departments to be separate factories or two or more factories to be a single factory.**—The State Government may, <sup>2</sup>\[on its own or] on an application made in this behalf by an occupier, direct, by an order in writing <sup>2</sup>\[and subject to such conditions as it may deem fit], that for all or any of the purposes of this Act different departments or branches of a factory of the occupier specified in the application shall be treated as separate factories or that two or more factories of the occupier specified in the application shall be treated as a single factory:]
items.value: <sup>1</sup>[4. Power to declare different departments to be separate factories or two or more factories to be a single factory.—The State Government may, <sup>2</sup>[on its own or] on an application made in this behalf by an occupier, direct, by an order in writing <sup>2</sup>[and subject to such conditions as it may deem fit], that for all or any of the purposes of this Act different departments or branches of a factory of the occupier specified in the application shall be treated as separate factories or that two or more factories of the occupier specified in the application shall be treated as a single factory:]

items.md: <sup>3</sup>\[Provided that no order under this section shall be made by the State Government on its own motion unless an opportunity of being heard is given to the occupier.]
items.value: <sup>3</sup>[Provided that no order under this section shall be made by the State Government on its own motion unless an opportunity of being heard is given to the occupier.]

items.md: **5. Power to exempt during public emergency.**—In any case of public emergency the State Government may, by notification in the Official Gazette, exempt any factory or class or description of factories from all or any of the provisions of this Act <sup>4</sup>\[except section 67] for such period and subject to such conditions as it may think fit:
items.value: 5. Power to exempt during public emergency.—In any case of public emergency the State Government may, by notification in the Official Gazette, exempt any factory or class or description of factories from all or any of the provisions of this Act <sup>4</sup>[except section 67] for such period and subject to such conditions as it may think fit:

items.md: Provided that no such notification shall be made for a period exceeding three months at a time.
items.value: Provided that no such notification shall be made for a period exceeding three months at a time.

items.md: <sup>5</sup>\[*Explanation.*—For the purposes of this section “public emergency” means a grave emergency whereby the security of India or of any part of the territory thereof is threatened, whether by war or external aggression or internal disturbance.]
items.value: <sup>5</sup>[Explanation.—For the purposes of this section “public emergency” means a grave emergency whereby the security of India or of any part of the territory thereof is threatened, whether by war or external aggression or internal disturbance.]

items.md: (3) Where a State Government or a Chief Inspector refuses to grant permission to the site, construction or extension of a factory or to the registration and licensing of a factory, the applicant may within thirty days of the date of such refusal appeal to the Central Government if the decision appealed from was of the State Government and to the State Government in any other case.

items.value: Explanation.—A factory shall not be deemed to be extended within the meaning of this section by reason only of the replacement of any plant or machinery or within such limits as may be prescribed, of the addition of any plant or machinery <sup>3</sup>[if such replacement or addition does not reduce the minimum clear space required for safe working around the plant or machinery or adversely affect the environmental conditions from the evolution or emission of steam, heat or dust or fumes injurious to health].

items.md: <sup>3</sup>\[**7A. General duties of the occupier.**—(1) Every occupier shall ensure, so far as is reasonably practicable, the health, safety and welfare of all workers while they are at work in the factory.
items.value: <sup>3</sup>[7A. General duties of the occupier.—(1) Every occupier shall ensure, so far as is reasonably practicable, the health, safety and welfare of all workers while they are at work in the factory.

items.md: (2) Without prejudice to the generality of the provisions of sub-section (1), the matters to which such duty extends, shall include—
items.value: (2) Without prejudice to the generality of the provisions of sub-section (1), the matters to which such duty extends, shall include—

items.md: (*a*) the provision and maintenance of plant and systems of work in the factory that are safe and without risks to health;
items.value: (a) the provision and maintenance of plant and systems of work in the factory that are safe and without risks to health;

items.md: (*b*) the arrangements in the factory for ensuring safety and absence of risks to health in connection with the use, handling, storage and transport of articles and substances;
items.value: (b) the arrangements in the factory for ensuring safety and absence of risks to health in connection with the use, handling, storage and transport of articles and substances;

items.md: (*c*) the provisions of such information, instruction, training and supervision as are necessary to ensure the health and safety of all workers at work;
items.value: (c) the provisions of such information, instruction, training and supervision as are necessary to ensure the health and safety of all workers at work;

items.md: (*d*) the maintenance of all places of work in the factory in a condition that is safe and without risks to health and the provision and maintenance of such means of access to, and egress from, such places as are safe and without such risks;
items.value: (d) the maintenance of all places of work in the factory in a condition that is safe and without risks to health and the provision and maintenance of such means of access to, and egress from, such places as are safe and without such risks;

items.md: (*e*) the provision, maintenance or monitoring of such working environment in the factory for the workers that is safe, without risks to health and adequate as regards facilities and arrangements for their welfare at work.
items.value: (e) the provision, maintenance or monitoring of such working environment in the factory for the workers that is safe, without risks to health and adequate as regards facilities and arrangements for their welfare at work.

items.md: (3) Except in such cases as may be prescribed, every occupier shall prepare, and, as often as may be appropriate, revise, a written statement of his general policy with respect to the health and safety of the workers at work and the organisation and arrangements for the time being in force for carrying out that policy, and to bring the statement and any revision thereof to the notice of all the workers in such manner as may be prescribed.
items.value: (3) Except in such cases as may be prescribed, every occupier shall prepare, and, as often as may be appropriate, revise, a written statement of his general policy with respect to the health and safety of the workers at work and the organisation and arrangements for the time being in force for carrying out that policy, and to bring the statement and any revision thereof to the notice of all the workers in such manner as may be prescribed.

items.md: (2) Every person, who undertakes to design or manufacture any article for use in any factory may carry out or arrange for the carrying out of necessary research with a view to the discovery and, so far as is reasonably practicable, the elimination or minimisation of any risks to the health or safety of the workers to which the design or article may give rise.
items.value: (2) Every person, who undertakes to design or manufacture any article for use in any factory may carry out or arrange for the carrying out of necessary research with a view to the discovery and, so far as is reasonably practicable, the elimination or minimisation of any risks to the health or safety of the workers to which the design or article may give rise.

items.md: (3) Nothing contained in sub-sections (1) and (2) shall be construed to require a person to repeat the testing, examination or research which has been carried out otherwise than by him or at his instance in so far as it is reasonable for him to rely on the results thereof for the purposes of the said sub-sections.
items.value: (3) Nothing contained in sub-sections (1) and (2) shall be construed to require a person to repeat the testing, examination or research which has been carried out otherwise than by him or at his instance in so far as it is reasonable for him to rely on the results thereof for the purposes of the said sub-sections.

items.md: (4) Any duty imposed on any person by sub-sections (1) and (2) shall extend only to things done in the course of business carried on by him and to matters within his control.
items.value: (4) Any duty imposed on any person by sub-sections (1) and (2) shall extend only to things done in the course of business carried on by him and to matters within his control.

items.md: (5) Where a person designs, manufactures, imports or supplies an article on the basis of a written undertaking by the user of such article to take the steps specified in such undertaking to ensure, so far as is reasonably practicable, that the article will be safe and without risks to the health of the workers when properly used, the undertaking shall have the effect of relieving the person designing, manufacturing, importing or supplying the article from the duty imposed by clause (*a*) of sub-section (1) to such extent as is reasonable having regard to the terms of the undertaking.
items.value: (5) Where a person designs, manufactures, imports or supplies an article on the basis of a written undertaking by the user of such article to take the steps specified in such undertaking to ensure, so far as is reasonably practicable, that the article will be safe and without risks to the health of the workers when properly used, the undertaking shall have the effect of relieving the person designing, manufacturing, importing or supplying the article from the duty imposed by clause (a) of sub-section (1) to such extent as is reasonable having regard to the terms of the undertaking.

items.md: (6) For the purposes of this section, an article is not to be regarded as properly used if it is used without regard to any information or advice relating to its use which has been made available by the person who has designed, manufactured, imported or supplied the article.
items.value: (6) For the purposes of this section, an article is not to be regarded as properly used if it is used without regard to any information or advice relating to its use which has been made available by the person who has designed, manufactured, imported or supplied the article.

items.md: **8. Inspectors.**—(1) The State Government may, by notification in the Official Gazette, appoint such persons as possess the prescribed qualification to be Inspectors for the purposes of this Act and may assign to them such local limits as it may think fit.
items.value: 8. Inspectors.—(1) The State Government may, by notification in the Official Gazette, appoint such persons as possess the prescribed qualification to be Inspectors for the purposes of this Act and may assign to them such local limits as it may think fit.

items.md: (2) The State Government may, by notification in the Official Gazette, appoint any person to be a Chief Inspector who shall, in addition to the powers conferred on a Chief Inspector under this Act, exercise the powers of an Inspector throughout the State.
items.value: (2) The State Government may, by notification in the Official Gazette, appoint any person to be a Chief Inspector who shall, in addition to the powers conferred on a Chief Inspector under this Act, exercise the powers of an Inspector throughout the State.

items.md: **10. Certifying surgeons.**—(1) The State Government may appoint qualified medical practitioners to be certifying surgeons for the purposes of this Act within such local limits or for such factory or class or description of factories as it may assign to them respectively.
items.value: 10. Certifying surgeons.—(1) The State Government may appoint qualified medical practitioners to be certifying surgeons for the purposes of this Act within such local limits or for such factory or class or description of factories as it may assign to them respectively.

items.md: (2) A certifying surgeon may, with the approval of the State Government, authorise any qualified medical practitioner to exercise any of his powers under this Act for such period as the certifying surgeon may specify and subject to such conditions as the State Government may think fit to impose, and references in this Act to a certifying surgeon shall be deemed to include references to any qualified medical practitioner when so authorised.
items.value: (2) A certifying surgeon may, with the approval of the State Government, authorise any qualified medical practitioner to exercise any of his powers under this Act for such period as the certifying surgeon may specify and subject to such conditions as the State Government may think fit to impose, and references in this Act to a certifying surgeon shall be deemed to include references to any qualified medical practitioner when so authorised.

items.md: # CHAPTER III
items.value: CHAPTER III

items.md: # HEALTH
items.value: HEALTH

items.md: **11. Cleanliness.**—(1) Every factory shall be kept clean and free from effluvia arising from any drain, privy or other nuisance, and in particular—
items.value: 11. Cleanliness.—(1) Every factory shall be kept clean and free from effluvia arising from any drain, privy or other nuisance, and in particular—

items.md: (a) accumulation of dirt and refuse shall be removed daily by sweeping or by any other effective method from the floors and benches of workrooms and from staircases and passages, and disposed of in a suitable manner;
items.value: (a) accumulation of dirt and refuse shall be removed daily by sweeping or by any other effective method from the floors and benches of workrooms and from staircases and passages, and disposed of in a suitable manner;

items.md: (b) the floor of every workroom shall be cleaned at least once in every week by washing, using disinfectant, where necessary, or by some other effective method;
items.value: (b) the floor of every workroom shall be cleaned at least once in every week by washing, using disinfectant, where necessary, or by some other effective method;

items.md: (c) where a floor is liable to become wet in the course of any manufacturing process to such extent as is capable of being drained, effective means of drainage shall be provided and maintained;
items.value: (c) where a floor is liable to become wet in the course of any manufacturing process to such extent as is capable of being drained, effective means of drainage shall be provided and maintained;

items.md: (d) all inside walls and partitions, all ceilings or tops of rooms and all walls, sides and tops of passages and staircases shall—
items.value: (d) all inside walls and partitions, all ceilings or tops of rooms and all walls, sides and tops of passages and staircases shall—

items.md: **13. Ventilation and temperature.**—(1) Effective and suitable provision shall be made in ever factory for securing and maintaining in every workroom—
items.value: 13. Ventilation and temperature.—(1) Effective and suitable provision shall be made in ever factory for securing and maintaining in every workroom—

items.md: (a) adequate ventilation by the circulation of fresh air, and
items.value: (a) adequate ventilation by the circulation of fresh air, and

items.md: (b) such a temperature as will secure to workers therein reasonable conditions of comfort and prevent injury to health;
items.value: (b) such a temperature as will secure to workers therein reasonable conditions of comfort and prevent injury to health;

items.md: and in particular,—
items.value: and in particular,—

items.md: (i) walls and roofs shall be of such material and so designed that such temperature shall not be exceeded bill kept as tow as practicable;

items.md: (ii) where the nature of the work carried on in the factory involves, or is likely to involve the production of excessively high temperatures, such adequate measures as are practicable shall be taken to protect the workers therefrom, by separating the process which produces such temperatures from the workroom, by insulating the hot parts or by other effective means.

items.md: **14. Dust and fume.**—(1) In every factory in which, by reason of the manufacturing process carried on, there is given off any dust or fume or other impurity of such a nature and to such an extent as is likely to be injurious or offensive to the workers employed therein, or any dust in substantial quantities, effective measures shall be taken to prevent its inhalation and accumulation in any workroom, and if any exhaust appliance is necessary for this purpose, it shall be applied as near as possible to the point of origin of the dust, fume or other impurity, and such point shall be enclosed so far as possible.
items.value: 14. Dust and fume.—(1) In every factory in which, by reason of the manufacturing process carried on, there is given off any dust or fume or other impurity of such a nature and to such an extent as is likely to be injurious or offensive to the workers employed therein, or any dust in substantial quantities, effective measures shall be taken to prevent its inhalation and accumulation in any workroom, and if any exhaust appliance is necessary for this purpose, it shall be applied as near as possible to the point of origin of the dust, fume or other impurity, and such point shall be enclosed so far as possible.

items.md: **15. Artificial humidification.**—(1) In respect of all factories in which the humidity of the air is artificially increased, the State Government may make rules,—
items.value: 15. Artificial humidification.—(1) In respect of all factories in which the humidity of the air is artificially increased, the State Government may make rules,—

items.md: (a) prescribing standards of humidification;
items.value: (a) prescribing standards of humidification;

items.md: (b) regulating the methods used for artificially increasing the humidity of the air;
items.value: (b) regulating the methods used for artificially increasing the humidity of the air;

items.md: (c) directing prescribed tests for determining the humidity of the air to be correctly carried out and recorded;
items.value: (c) directing prescribed tests for determining the humidity of the air to be correctly carried out and recorded;

items.md: (d) prescribing methods to be adopted for securing adequate ventilation and cooling of the air in the workrooms.
items.value: (d) prescribing methods to be adopted for securing adequate ventilation and cooling of the air in the workrooms.

items.md: (2) In any factory in which the humidity of the air is artificially increased, the water used for the purpose shall be taken from a public supply, or other source of drinking water, or shall be effectively purified before it is so used.

items.md: **16. Overcrowding.**—(1) No room in any factory shall be overcrowded to an extent injurious to the health of the workers employed therein.
items.value: 16. Overcrowding.—(1) No room in any factory shall be overcrowded to an extent injurious to the health of the workers employed therein.

items.md: (2) Without prejudice to the generality of sub-section (1) there shall be in every workroom of a factory in existence on the date of the commencement of this Act at least<sup>1</sup>\[9.9 cubic meters] and of a factory built after the commencement of this Act at least<sup>2</sup>\[14.2 cubic meters] of space for every worker employed therein, and for the purposes of this sub-section no account shall be taken of any space which is more than<sup>3</sup>\[4.2 meters] above the level of the floor of the room.

items.md: (3) If the Chief Inspector by order in writing so requires, there shall be posted in each workroom of a factory a notice specifying the maximum number of workers who may, in compliance with the provisions of this section, be employed in the room.

items.md: **17. Lighting.**—(1) In every pan of a factory where workers are working of passing there shall be provided and maintained sufficient and suitable lighting, natural or artificial, or both.

items.md: **18. Drinking water.**—(1) In every factory effective arrangements shall be made to provide and maintain at suitable points conveniently situated for all workers employed therein a sufficient supply of wholesome drinking water.
items.value: 18. Drinking water.—(1) In every factory effective arrangements shall be made to provide and maintain at suitable points conveniently situated for all workers employed therein a sufficient supply of wholesome drinking water.

items.md: (2) All such points shall be legibly marked "drinking water" in a language understood by a majority of the workers employed in the factory, and no such point shall be situated within <sup>1</sup>\[six meters of any washing place, urinal, latrine, spittoon, open drain carrying sullage or effluent or any other source of contamination] unless a shorter distance is approved in writing by the Chief Inspector.
items.value: (2) All such points shall be legibly marked "drinking water" in a language understood by a majority of the workers employed in the factory, and no such point shall be situated within <sup>1</sup>[six meters of any washing place, urinal, latrine, spittoon, open drain carrying sullage or effluent or any other source of contamination] unless a shorter distance is approved in writing by the Chief Inspector.

items.md: (3) In every factory wherein more than two hundred and fifty workers are ordinarily employed, provision shall be made for cool drinking water during hot weather by effective means and for distribution thereof.
items.value: (3) In every factory wherein more than two hundred and fifty workers are ordinarily employed, provision shall be made for cool drinking water during hot weather by effective means and for distribution thereof.

items.md: (4) In respect of all factories or any class or description of factories the State Government may make rules for securing compliance with the provisions of sub-sections (1), (2) and (3) and for the examination by prescribed authorities of the supply and distribution of drinking water in factories.
items.value: (4) In respect of all factories or any class or description of factories the State Government may make rules for securing compliance with the provisions of sub-sections (1), (2) and (3) and for the examination by prescribed authorities of the supply and distribution of drinking water in factories.

items.md: **19. Latrines and urinals.**—(1) In every factory—
items.value: 19. Latrines and urinals.—(1) In every factory—

items.md: (a) sufficient latrine and urinal accommodation of prescribed types shall be provided conveniently situated and accessible to workers at all times while they are at factory;
items.value: (a) sufficient latrine and urinal accommodation of prescribed types shall be provided conveniently situated and accessible to workers at all times while they are at factory;

items.md: (b) separate enclosed accommodation shall be provided for male and female workers;

items.md: (c) such accommodation shall be adequately lighted and ventilated, and no latrine or urinal shall, unless specially exempted in writing by the Chief Inspector, communicate with any work room except through an intervening open space or ventilated passage;
items.value: (c) such accommodation shall be adequately lighted and ventilated, and no latrine or urinal shall, unless specially exempted in writing by the Chief Inspector, communicate with any work room except through an intervening open space or ventilated passage;

items.md: (d) all such accommodation shall be maintained in a clean and sanitary condition at all times;
items.value: (d) all such accommodation shall be maintained in a clean and sanitary condition at all times;

items.md: (e) sweepers shall be employed whose primary duty it would be to keep clean latrines, urinals and washing places.
items.value: (e) sweepers shall be employed whose primary duty it would be to keep clean latrines, urinals and washing places.

items.md: **21. Fencing of machinery.**—(1) In every factory the following, namely:—
items.value: 21. Fencing of machinery.—(1) In every factory the following, namely:—

items.md: (i) every moving part of a prime mover and every flywheel connected to a prime mover, whether the prime mover or flywheel is in the engine house or not;
items.value: (i) every moving part of a prime mover and every flywheel connected to a prime mover, whether the prime mover or flywheel is in the engine house or not;

items.md: (ii) the headrace and tailrace of every water-wheel and water turbine:

items.md: (iii) any part of a stock-bar which projects beyond the head stock of a lathe; and
items.value: (iii) any part of a stock-bar which projects beyond the head stock of a lathe; and

items.md: (iv) unless they are in such position or of such construction as to be safe to every person employed in the factory as they would be if they were securely fenced, the following, namely:—
items.value: (iv) unless they are in such position or of such construction as to be safe to every person employed in the factory as they would be if they were securely fenced, the following, namely:—

items.md: (a) every part of an electric generator, a motor or rotary converter;

items.md: (b) every part of transmission machinery; and

items.md: **23. Employment of young persons on dangerous machines.**—(1) No young person <sup>2</sup>\[shall be required or allowed to work] at any machine to which this section applies, unless he has been fully instructed as to the dangers arising in connection with the machine and the precautions to be observed and—
items.value: 23. Employment of young persons on dangerous machines.—(1) No young person <sup>2</sup>[shall be required or allowed to work] at any machine to which this section applies, unless he has been fully instructed as to the dangers arising in connection with the machine and the precautions to be observed and—

items.md: (*a*) has received sufficient training in work at the machine, or
items.value: (a) has received sufficient training in work at the machine, or

items.md: (*b*) is under adequate supervision by a person who has a thorough knowledge and experience of the machine.

items.md: **25. Self-acting machines.**—No traversing part of a self-acting machine in any factory and no material carried thereon shall, if the space over which it runs is a space over which any person is liable to pass, whether in the course of his employment or otherwise, be allowed to run on its outward or inward traverse within a distance of <sup>2</sup>\[forty-five centimeters] from any fixed structure which is not part of the machine:
items.value: 25. Self-acting machines.—No traversing part of a self-acting machine in any factory and no material carried thereon shall, if the space over which it runs is a space over which any person is liable to pass, whether in the course of his employment or otherwise, be allowed to run on its outward or inward traverse within a distance of <sup>2</sup>[forty-five centimeters] from any fixed structure which is not part of the machine:

items.md: **26. Casing of new machinery.**—(1) In all machinery driven by power and installed in any factory after the commencement of this Act,—
items.value: 26. Casing of new machinery.—(1) In all machinery driven by power and installed in any factory after the commencement of this Act,—

items.md: (*a*) every set screw, bolt or key on any revolving shaft, spindle, wheel or pinion shall be so sunk, encased or otherwise effectively guarded as to prevent danger;
items.value: (a) every set screw, bolt or key on any revolving shaft, spindle, wheel or pinion shall be so sunk, encased or otherwise effectively guarded as to prevent danger;

items.md: (*b*) all spur, worm and other toothed or friction gearing which does not require frequent adjustment while in motion shall be completely encased, unless it is so situated as to be as safe as it would be if it were completely encased.
items.value: (b) all spur, worm and other toothed or friction gearing which does not require frequent adjustment while in motion shall be completely encased, unless it is so situated as to be as safe as it would be if it were completely encased.

items.md: (2) Whoever sells or lets on hire or, as agent of a seller or hirer, causes or procures to be sold or let on hire, for use in a factory any machinery driven by power which does not comply with the provisions of <sup>3</sup>\[sub-section (1) or any rules made under sub-section (3)], shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term which may extend to three months or with fine which may extend to five hundred rupees or with both.
items.value: (2) Whoever sells or lets on hire or, as agent of a seller or hirer, causes or procures to be sold or let on hire, for use in a factory any machinery driven by power which does not comply with the provisions of <sup>3</sup>[sub-section (1) or any rules made under sub-section (3)], shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term which may extend to three months or with fine which may extend to five hundred rupees or with both.

items.md: **27. Prohibition of employment of women and children near cotton-openers.**—No woman or child shall be employed in any part of a factory for pressing cotton in which a cotton opener is at work:

items.md: **33. Pits, sumps openings in floors, etc.**—(1) In every factory every fixed vessel, sump, tank, pit or opening in the ground or in a floor which, by reason of its depth, situation, construction or contents, is or may be a source of danger, shall be either securely covered or securely fenced.

items.md: **34. Excessive weights.**—(1) No person shall be employed in any factory to lift, carry or move any load so heavy as to be likely to cause him injury.
items.value: 34. Excessive weights.—(1) No person shall be employed in any factory to lift, carry or move any load so heavy as to be likely to cause him injury.

items.md: (2) The State Government may make rules prescribing the maximum weights which may be lifted, carried or moved by adult men, adult women, adolescents and children employed in factories or in any class or description of factories or in carrying or any specified process.

items.md: **35. Protection of eyes.**—In respect of any such manufacturing process carried on in any factory as may be prescribed, being a process which involves—

items.md: (a) risk of injury to the eyes from particles or fragments thrown off in the course of the process, or

items.md: <sup>2</sup>\[**36. Precautions against dangerous fumes, gases, etc.**—(1) No person shall be required or allowed to enter any chamber, tank, vat, pit, pipe, flue or other confined space in any factory in which any gas, fume vapour or dust is likely to be present to such an extent as to involve risk to persons being overcome thereby, unless it is provided with a manhole of adequate size or other effective means of egress.
items.value: <sup>2</sup>[36. Precautions against dangerous fumes, gases, etc.—(1) No person shall be required or allowed to enter any chamber, tank, vat, pit, pipe, flue or other confined space in any factory in which any gas, fume vapour or dust is likely to be present to such an extent as to involve risk to persons being overcome thereby, unless it is provided with a manhole of adequate size or other effective means of egress.

items.md: (2) No person shall be required or allowed to enter any confined space as is referred to in sub-section (1), until all practicable measures have been taken to remove any gas, fume, vapour or dust, which may be present so as to bring its level within the permissible limits and to prevent any ingress of such gas, fume, vapour or dust and unless—
items.value: (2) No person shall be required or allowed to enter any confined space as is referred to in sub-section (1), until all practicable measures have been taken to remove any gas, fume, vapour or dust, which may be present so as to bring its level within the permissible limits and to prevent any ingress of such gas, fume, vapour or dust and unless—

items.md: (a) a certificate in writing has been given by a competent person, based on a test carried out by himself that the space is reasonably free from dangerous gas, fume, vapour or dust; or

items.md: (b) such person is wearing suitable breathing apparatus and a belt securely attached to a rope the free end of which is held by a person outside the confined space.]

items.md: **37. Explosive or inflammable dust, gas, etc.**—(1) Where in any factory any manufacturing process produces dust, gas, fume or vapour of such character and to such extent as to be likely to explode on ignition, all practicable measure shall be taken to prevent any such explosion by—
items.value: 37. Explosive or inflammable dust, gas, etc.—(1) Where in any factory any manufacturing process produces dust, gas, fume or vapour of such character and to such extent as to be likely to explode on ignition, all practicable measure shall be taken to prevent any such explosion by—

items.md: *(a)* effective enclosure of the plant or machinery used in the process;
items.value: (a) effective enclosure of the plant or machinery used in the process;

items.md: *(b)* removal or prevention of the accumulation of such dust, gas, fume or vapour;
items.value: (b) removal or prevention of the accumulation of such dust, gas, fume or vapour;

items.md: *(c)* exclusion or effective enclosure of all possible sources of ignition.
items.value: (c) exclusion or effective enclosure of all possible sources of ignition.

items.md: (2) Where in any factory the plant or machinery used in a process such as is referred to in sub-section (1) is not so constructed as to withstand the probable pressure which such an explosion as aforesaid would produce, all practicable measures shall be taken to restrict the spread and effects of the explosion by the provision in the plant or machinery of chokes, baffles, vents or other effective appliances.
items.value: (2) Where in any factory the plant or machinery used in a process such as is referred to in sub-section (1) is not so constructed as to withstand the probable pressure which such an explosion as aforesaid would produce, all practicable measures shall be taken to restrict the spread and effects of the explosion by the provision in the plant or machinery of chokes, baffles, vents or other effective appliances.

items.md: (3) Where any part of the plant or machinery in a factory contains any explosive or inflammable gas or vapour under pressure greater than atmospheric pressure, that part shall not be opened except in accordance with the following provisions, namely:—
items.value: (3) Where any part of the plant or machinery in a factory contains any explosive or inflammable gas or vapour under pressure greater than atmospheric pressure, that part shall not be opened except in accordance with the following provisions, namely:—

items.md: *(a)* before the fastening of any joint of any pipe connected with the part or the fastening of the cover of any opening into the part is loosened, any flow of the gas or vapour into the part of any such pipe shall be effectively stopped by a stop-valve or other means;
items.value: (a) before the fastening of any joint of any pipe connected with the part or the fastening of the cover of any opening into the part is loosened, any flow of the gas or vapour into the part of any such pipe shall be effectively stopped by a stop-valve or other means;

items.md: *(b)* before any such fastening as aforesaid is removed, all practicable measures shall be taken to reduce the pressure of the gas or vapour in the part or pipe to atmospheric pressure;
items.value: (b) before any such fastening as aforesaid is removed, all practicable measures shall be taken to reduce the pressure of the gas or vapour in the part or pipe to atmospheric pressure;

items.md: *(c)* where any such fastening as aforesaid has been loosened or removed effective measures shall be taken or prevent any explosive or inflammable gas or vapour from entering the part or pipe until the fastening has been secured, or the case may be, securely replaced;
items.value: (c) where any such fastening as aforesaid has been loosened or removed effective measures shall be taken or prevent any explosive or inflammable gas or vapour from entering the part or pipe until the fastening has been secured, or the case may be, securely replaced;

items.md: Provided that the provisions of this sub-section shall not apply in the case of plant or machinery installed in the open air.
items.value: Provided that the provisions of this sub-section shall not apply in the case of plant or machinery installed in the open air.

items.md: (4) No plant, tank or vessel which contains or has contained any explosive or inflammable substance shall be subjected in any factory to any welding, brazing, soldering or cutting operation which involves the application of heat unless adequate measures have first been taken to remove such substance and any fumes arising therefrom or to render such substance and fumes non-explosive or non-inflammable, and no such substance shall be allowed to enter such plant, tank or vessel after any such operation until the metal has cooled sufficiently to prevent any risk of igniting the substance.
items.value: (4) No plant, tank or vessel which contains or has contained any explosive or inflammable substance shall be subjected in any factory to any welding, brazing, soldering or cutting operation which involves the application of heat unless adequate measures have first been taken to remove such substance and any fumes arising therefrom or to render such substance and fumes non-explosive or non-inflammable, and no such substance shall be allowed to enter such plant, tank or vessel after any such operation until the metal has cooled sufficiently to prevent any risk of igniting the substance.

items.md: (5) The State Government may by rules exempt, subject to such conditions as may be prescribed, any factory or class or description of factories from compliance with all or any of the provisions of this section.
items.value: (5) The State Government may by rules exempt, subject to such conditions as may be prescribed, any factory or class or description of factories from compliance with all or any of the provisions of this section.

items.md: <sup>1</sup>\[**38. Precautions in case of fire.**—(1) In every factory, all practicable me assures shall he taken to prevent outbreak of fire and its spread, both internally and externally, and to provide and maintain—
items.value: <sup>1</sup>[38. Precautions in case of fire.—(1) In every factory, all practicable me assures shall he taken to prevent outbreak of fire and its spread, both internally and externally, and to provide and maintain—

items.md: *(a)* safe means of escape for all persons in the event of a fire, and
items.value: (a) safe means of escape for all persons in the event of a fire, and

items.md: *(b)* the necessary equipment and facilities for extinguishing fire.
items.value: (b) the necessary equipment and facilities for extinguishing fire.

items.md: (2) Effective me assures shall be taken to ensure that in every factory all the workers are familiar with the means of escape in case of fire and have been adequately trained in the routine to be followed in such cases.
items.value: (2) Effective me assures shall be taken to ensure that in every factory all the workers are familiar with the means of escape in case of fire and have been adequately trained in the routine to be followed in such cases.

items.md: (3) The State Government may make rules, in respect of any factory or class or description of factories, requiring the meassures to be adopted to give effect to the provisions of sub-section (1) and (2).
items.value: (3) The State Government may make rules, in respect of any factory or class or description of factories, requiring the meassures to be adopted to give effect to the provisions of sub-section (1) and (2).

items.md: **40. Safety of buildings and machinery.**—(1) If it appears to the Inspector that any building or part of a building or any part of the ways, machinery or plant in a factory is in such a condition that it is dangerous to human life or safety, he may serve on<sup>1</sup> \[the occupier or manager or both] of the factory an order in writing specifying the me assures which in his opinion should be adopted, and requiring them to be carried out before a specified date.
items.value: 40. Safety of buildings and machinery.—(1) If it appears to the Inspector that any building or part of a building or any part of the ways, machinery or plant in a factory is in such a condition that it is dangerous to human life or safety, he may serve on<sup>1</sup> [the occupier or manager or both] of the factory an order in writing specifying the me assures which in his opinion should be adopted, and requiring them to be carried out before a specified date.

items.md: (2) If it appears to the Inspector that the use of any building or part of a building or any part of the ways, machinery or plant in a factory involves imminent danger to human life or safety, he may serve on<sup>1</sup> \[the occupier or manager or both] of the factory an order in writing prohibiting its use until it has been properly repaired or altered.
items.value: (2) If it appears to the Inspector that the use of any building or part of a building or any part of the ways, machinery or plant in a factory involves imminent danger to human life or safety, he may serve on<sup>1</sup> [the occupier or manager or both] of the factory an order in writing prohibiting its use until it has been properly repaired or altered.

items.md: **41A. Constitution of Site Appraisal Committees.**—(1) The State Government may, for purposes of advising it to consider applications for grant of permission for the initial location of a factory involving a hazardous process or for the expansion of an such factory, appoint a Site Appraisal Committee consisting of—
items.value: 41A. Constitution of Site Appraisal Committees.—(1) The State Government may, for purposes of advising it to consider applications for grant of permission for the initial location of a factory involving a hazardous process or for the expansion of an such factory, appoint a Site Appraisal Committee consisting of—

items.md: (a) the Chief Inspector of the State who shall be its Chairman;
items.value: (a) the Chief Inspector of the State who shall be its Chairman;

items.md: (b) a representative of the Central Board for the Prevention and Control of Water Pollution appointed by the Central Government under section 3 of the Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974 (6 of 1974);
items.value: (b) a representative of the Central Board for the Prevention and Control of Water Pollution appointed by the Central Government under section 3 of the Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974 (6 of 1974);

items.md: (c) a representative of the Central Board for the Prevention and Control of Air Pollution referred to in section 3 of the Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1981 (14 of 1981);

items.md: (d) a representative of the State Board appointed under section 4 of the Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974 (6 of 1974);

items.value: (e) a representative of the State Board for the Prevention and Control of Air Pollution referred to in section 5 of the Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1981 (14 of 1981);

items.value: (f) a representative of the Department of Environment in the State;

items.value: (g) a representative of the Meteorological Department of the Government of India;

items.md: (h) an expert in the field of occupational health; and

items.md: (i) a representative of the Town Planning Department of the State Government,
items.value: (i) a representative of the Town Planning Department of the State Government,

items.md: and not more than five other members who may be co-opted by the State Government who shall be—
items.value: and not more than five other members who may be co-opted by the State Government who shall be—

items.md: (i) a scientist having specialised knowledge of the hazardous process which will be involved in the factory,

items.value: (iii) not more than three other persons as deemed fit by the State Government.

items.md: (2) The Site Appraisal Committee shall examine an application for the establishment of a factory involving hazardous process and make its recommendation to the State Government within a period of ninety days of the receipt of such application in the prescribed form.
items.value: (2) The Site Appraisal Committee shall examine an application for the establishment of a factory involving hazardous process and make its recommendation to the State Government within a period of ninety days of the receipt of such application in the prescribed form.

items.md: (3) Where any process relates to a factory owned or controlled by the Central Government or to a corporation or a company owned or controlled by the Central Government, the State Government shall co-opt in the Site Appraisal Committee a representative nominated by the Central Government as a member of that Committee.
items.value: (3) Where any process relates to a factory owned or controlled by the Central Government or to a corporation or a company owned or controlled by the Central Government, the State Government shall co-opt in the Site Appraisal Committee a representative nominated by the Central Government as a member of that Committee.

items.md: (4) The Site Appraisal Committee shall have power to call for any information from the person making an application for the establishment or expansion of a factory involving a hazardous process.
items.value: (4) The Site Appraisal Committee shall have power to call for any information from the person making an application for the establishment or expansion of a factory involving a hazardous process.

items.md: (5) Where the State Government has granted approval to an application for the establishment or expansion of a factory involving a hazardous process, it shall not be necessary for an applicant to obtain a further approval from the Central Board or the State Board established under the Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974 (6 of 1974) and the Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1981 (14 of 1981).
items.value: (5) Where the State Government has granted approval to an application for the establishment or expansion of a factory involving a hazardous process, it shall not be necessary for an applicant to obtain a further approval from the Central Board or the State Board established under the Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974 (6 of 1974) and the Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1981 (14 of 1981).

items.md: **41B. Compulsory disclosure of information by the occupier.**—(1) The occupier of every factory involving a hazardous process shall disclose in the manner prescribed all information regarding dangers, including health hazards and the measures to overcome such hazards arising from the exposure to or handling of the materials or substances in the manufacture, transportation, storage and other processes, to the workers employed in the factory, the Chief Inspector, the local authority within whose jurisdiction the factory is situate and the general public in the vicinity.
items.value: 41B. Compulsory disclosure of information by the occupier.—(1) The occupier of every factory involving a hazardous process shall disclose in the manner prescribed all information regarding dangers, including health hazards and the measures to overcome such hazards arising from the exposure to or handling of the materials or substances in the manufacture, transportation, storage and other processes, to the workers employed in the factory, the Chief Inspector, the local authority within whose jurisdiction the factory is situate and the general public in the vicinity.

items.md: (2) The occupier shall, at the time of registering the factory involving a hazardous process, lay down a detailed policy with respect to the health and safety of the workers employed therein and intimate such policy to the Chief Inspector and the local authority and, thereafter, at such intervals as may be prescribed, inform the Chief Inspector and the local authority of any change made in the said policy.
items.value: (2) The occupier shall, at the time of registering the factory involving a hazardous process, lay down a detailed policy with respect to the health and safety of the workers employed therein and intimate such policy to the Chief Inspector and the local authority and, thereafter, at such intervals as may be prescribed, inform the Chief Inspector and the local authority of any change made in the said policy.

items.md: (3) The information furnished under sub-section (1) shall include accurate information as to the quantity, specifications and other characteristics of wastes and the manner of their disposal.
items.value: (3) The information furnished under sub-section (1) shall include accurate information as to the quantity, specifications and other characteristics of wastes and the manner of their disposal.

items.md: (4) Every occupier shall, with the approval of the Chief Inspector, draw up an on-site emergency plan and detailed disaster control measures for his factory and make known to the workers employed therein and to the general public living in the vicinity of the factory the safety measures required to be taken in the event of an accident taking place.
items.value: (4) Every occupier shall, with the approval of the Chief Inspector, draw up an on-site emergency plan and detailed disaster control measures for his factory and make known to the workers employed therein and to the general public living in the vicinity of the factory the safety measures required to be taken in the event of an accident taking place.

items.md: (5) Every occupier of a factory shall,—

items.md: inform the Chief Inspector of the nature and details of the process in such form and in such manner as may be prescribed.

items.md: (6) Where any occupier of a factory contravenes the provisions of sub-section (5), the licence issued under section 6 to such factory shall, notwithstanding any penalty to which the occupier or factory shall be subjected to under the provisions of this Act, be liable for cancellation.
items.value: (6) Where any occupier of a factory contravenes the provisions of sub-section (5), the licence issued under section 6 to such factory shall, notwithstanding any penalty to which the occupier or factory shall be subjected to under the provisions of this Act, be liable for cancellation.

items.md: (7) The occupier of a factory involving a hazardous process shall, with the previous approval of the Chief Inspector, lay down measures for the handling, usage, transportation and storage of hazardous substances inside the factory premises and the disposal of such substances outside the factory premises and publicise them in the manner prescribed among the workers and the general public living in the vicinity.
items.value: (7) The occupier of a factory involving a hazardous process shall, with the previous approval of the Chief Inspector, lay down measures for the handling, usage, transportation and storage of hazardous substances inside the factory premises and the disposal of such substances outside the factory premises and publicise them in the manner prescribed among the workers and the general public living in the vicinity.

items.md: **41C. Specific responsibility of the occupier in relation to hazardous processes.**—Every occupier of a factory involving any hazardous process shall—
items.value: 41C. Specific responsibility of the occupier in relation to hazardous processes.—Every occupier of a factory involving any hazardous process shall—

items.md: (a) maintain accurate and up-to-date health records or, as the case may be, medical records, of the workers in the factory who are exposed to any chemical, toxic or any other harmful substances which are manufactured, stored, handled or transported and such records shall be accessible to the workers subject to such conditions as may be prescribed;
items.value: (a) maintain accurate and up-to-date health records or, as the case may be, medical records, of the workers in the factory who are exposed to any chemical, toxic or any other harmful substances which are manufactured, stored, handled or transported and such records shall be accessible to the workers subject to such conditions as may be prescribed;

items.md: (b) appoint persons who possess qualifications and experience in handling hazardous substances and are competent to supervise such handling within the factory and to provide at the working place all the necessary facilities for protecting the workers in the manner prescribed:
items.value: (b) appoint persons who possess qualifications and experience in handling hazardous substances and are competent to supervise such handling within the factory and to provide at the working place all the necessary facilities for protecting the workers in the manner prescribed:

items.md: > Provided that where any question arises as to the qualifications and experience of a person so appointed, the decision of the Chief Inspector shall be final;

items.md: (c) provide for medical examination of every worker—

* (b) while continuing in such job, and after he has ceased to work in such job, at intervals not exceeding twelve months, in such manner as may be prescribed.

items.md: **41D. Power of Central Government to appoint Inquiry Committee.**—(1) The Central Government may, in the event of the occurrence of an extraordinary situation involving a factory engaged in a hazardous process, appoint an Inquiry Committee to inquire into the standards of health and safety observed in the factory with a view to finding out the causes of any failure or neglect in the adoption of any measures or standards prescribed for the health and safety of the workers employed in the factory or the general public affected, or likely to be affected, due to such failure or neglect and for the prevention and recurrence of such extraordinary situations in future in such factory or elsewhere.
items.value: 41D. Power of Central Government to appoint Inquiry Committee.—(1) The Central Government may, in the event of the occurrence of an extraordinary situation involving a factory engaged in a hazardous process, appoint an Inquiry Committee to inquire into the standards of health and safety observed in the factory with a view to finding out the causes of any failure or neglect in the adoption of any measures or standards prescribed for the health and safety of the workers employed in the factory or the general public affected, or likely to be affected, due to such failure or neglect and for the prevention and recurrence of such extraordinary situations in future in such factory or elsewhere.

items.md: (2) The Committee appointed under sub-section (1) shall consist of a Chairman and two other members and the terms of reference of the Committee and the tenure of office of its members shall be such as may be determined by the Central Government according to the requirements of the situation.
items.value: (2) The Committee appointed under sub-section (1) shall consist of a Chairman and two other members and the terms of reference of the Committee and the tenure of office of its members shall be such as may be determined by the Central Government according to the requirements of the situation.

items.md: (3) The recommendations of the Committee shall be advisory in the nature.
items.value: (3) The recommendations of the Committee shall be advisory in the nature.

items.md: **41E. Emergency standards.**—(1) Where the Central Government is satisfied that no standards of safety have been prescribed in respect of a hazardous process or class of hazardous processes, or where the standards so prescribed are inadequate, it may direct the Director-General of Factory Advice Service and Labour Institutes or any institution specialised in matters relating to standards of safety in hazardous processes, to lay down emergency standards for enforcement of suitable standards in respect of such hazardous processes.
items.value: 41E. Emergency standards.—(1) Where the Central Government is satisfied that no standards of safety have been prescribed in respect of a hazardous process or class of hazardous processes, or where the standards so prescribed are inadequate, it may direct the Director-General of Factory Advice Service and Labour Institutes or any institution specialised in matters relating to standards of safety in hazardous processes, to lay down emergency standards for enforcement of suitable standards in respect of such hazardous processes.

items.md: (2) The emergency standards laid down under sub-section (1) shall, until they are incorporated in the rules made under this Act, be enforceable and have the same effect as if they had been incorporated in the rules made under this Act.
items.value: (2) The emergency standards laid down under sub-section (1) shall, until they are incorporated in the rules made under this Act, be enforceable and have the same effect as if they had been incorporated in the rules made under this Act.

items.md: **41F. Permissible limits of exposure of chemical and toxic substances.**—(1) The maximum permissible threshold limits of exposure of chemical and toxic substances in manufacturing processes (whether hazardous or otherwise) in any factory shall be of the value indicated in the Second Schedule.
items.value: 41F. Permissible limits of exposure of chemical and toxic substances.—(1) The maximum permissible threshold limits of exposure of chemical and toxic substances in manufacturing processes (whether hazardous or otherwise) in any factory shall be of the value indicated in the Second Schedule.

items.md: (2) The Central Government may, at any time, for the purpose of giving effect to any scientific proof obtained from specialised institutions or experts in the field by notification in the Official Gazette, make suitable changes in the said Schedule.
items.value: (2) The Central Government may, at any time, for the purpose of giving effect to any scientific proof obtained from specialised institutions or experts in the field by notification in the Official Gazette, make suitable changes in the said Schedule.

items.md: **41G. Workers’ participation in safety management.**—(1) The occupier shall, in every factory where a hazardous process takes place, or where hazardous substances are used or handled, set up a Safety Committee consisting of equal number of representatives of workers and management to promote co-operation between the workers and the management in maintaining proper safety and health at work and to review periodical the measures taken in that behalf:
items.value: 41G. Workers’ participation in safety management.—(1) The occupier shall, in every factory where a hazardous process takes place, or where hazardous substances are used or handled, set up a Safety Committee consisting of equal number of representatives of workers and management to promote co-operation between the workers and the management in maintaining proper safety and health at work and to review periodical the measures taken in that behalf:

items.md: Provided that the State Government may, by order in writing and for reasons to be recorded exempt the occupier of any factory or class of factories from setting up such Committee.
items.value: Provided that the State Government may, by order in writing and for reasons to be recorded exempt the occupier of any factory or class of factories from setting up such Committee.

items.md: (2) The composition of the Safety Committee, the tenure of office of its members and their right and duties shall be such as may be prescribed.
items.value: (2) The composition of the Safety Committee, the tenure of office of its members and their right and duties shall be such as may be prescribed.

items.md: **41H. Right of workers to warn about imminent danger.**—(1) Where the workers employed in any factory engaged in a hazardous process have reasonable apprehension that there is a likelihood of imminent danger to their lives or health due to any accident, they may bring the same to the notice of the occupier, agent, manager or any other person who is in charge of the factory or the process concerned directly or through their representatives in the Safety Committee and simultaneously bring the same to the notice of the Inspector.
items.value: 41H. Right of workers to warn about imminent danger.—(1) Where the workers employed in any factory engaged in a hazardous process have reasonable apprehension that there is a likelihood of imminent danger to their lives or health due to any accident, they may bring the same to the notice of the occupier, agent, manager or any other person who is in charge of the factory or the process concerned directly or through their representatives in the Safety Committee and simultaneously bring the same to the notice of the Inspector.

items.md: (2) It shall be the duty of such occupier, agent, manager or the person in charge of the factory or process to take immediate remedial action if he is satisfied about the existence of such imminent danger and send a report forthwith of the action taken to the nearest Inspector.
items.value: (2) It shall be the duty of such occupier, agent, manager or the person in charge of the factory or process to take immediate remedial action if he is satisfied about the existence of such imminent danger and send a report forthwith of the action taken to the nearest Inspector.

items.md: (3) If the occupier, agent, manager or the person in charge referred to in sub-section (2) is not satisfied about the existence of any imminent danger as apprehended by the workers, he shall, nevertheless, refer the matter forthwith to the nearest Inspector whose decision on the question of the existence of such imminent danger shall be final.]
items.value: (3) If the occupier, agent, manager or the person in charge referred to in sub-section (2) is not satisfied about the existence of any imminent danger as apprehended by the workers, he shall, nevertheless, refer the matter forthwith to the nearest Inspector whose decision on the question of the existence of such imminent danger shall be final.]

items.md: **43. Facilities for storing and drying clothing.**—The State Government may, in respect of any factory or class or description of factories, make rules requiring the provision therein of suitable places for keeping clothing not worn during working hours and for the drying of wet clothing.
items.value: 43. Facilities for storing and drying clothing.—The State Government may, in respect of any factory or class or description of factories, make rules requiring the provision therein of suitable places for keeping clothing not worn during working hours and for the drying of wet clothing.

items.md: **44. Facilities for sitting.**—(1) In every factory suitable arrangements for sitting shall be provided and maintained for all workers obliged to work in a standing position, in order that they may take advantage of any opportunities for rest which may occur in the course of their work.
items.value: 44. Facilities for sitting.—(1) In every factory suitable arrangements for sitting shall be provided and maintained for all workers obliged to work in a standing position, in order that they may take advantage of any opportunities for rest which may occur in the course of their work.

items.md: (2) If, in the opinion of the Chief Inspector, the workers in any factory engaged in a particular manufacturing process or working in a particular room are able to do their work efficiently in a sitting position, he may, by order in writing, require the occupier of the factory to provide before a specified date such seating arrangements as may be practicable for all workers so engaged or working.

items.md: (3) The State Government may, by notification in the Official Gazette, declare that the provisions of sub-section (1) shall not apply to any specified factory or class or description of factories or to any specified manufacturing process.

items.md: **45. First-aid appliances.**—(1) There shall in every factory be provided and maintained so as to be readily accessible during all working hours first-aid boxes or cupboards equipped with the prescribed contents, and the number of such boxes or cupboards to be provided and maintained shall not be less than one for every one hundred and fifty workers ordinarily employed <sup>1</sup>\[at any one time] in the factory.

items.md: **47. items.md: **48. Creches.**—(1) In every factory wherein more than <sup>2</sup>\[thirty women workers] are ordinarily employed there shall be provided and maintained a suitable room or rooms for the use of children under the age of six years of such women.
